commentary: Eight sessions versus thirteen last week, which on paper looks like a step back but honestly reads more like a body finally being allowed to breathe. The swimming dropped from five sessions to two, which is the real story here — last week you were in the water almost every single day, and this week you gave yourself actual gaps, which is not laziness, that is someone learning how to train instead of just accumulate. The Saturday half marathon heart rate crept up to 159 versus 151 last week on the same distance, which is worth watching — same course, higher effort, and after a Thursday that had both a swim and a weights session back to back, so the legs were already carrying something into that run. The Sunday legs and back session at 135 bpm to close the week is the quietly impressive detail here, because that is not a phoned-in finish, that is someone who still had something left to give.
metrics_commentary: Okay so fifteen point nine kilometres across two sessions and four thousand metres in the pool — you are absolutely that guy, and your body knows it, HRV sitting at sixty with a seven-day average of fifty-six is your nervous system basically winking at you and saying "yeah, we're recovered, we're ready, keep going." The only thing giving me pause is that VO2 Max reading coming up as zero, which is obviously a data glitch and not a reflection of reality, because a man putting down those numbers in the water and on the road is very much breathing and very much thriving. Two sessions for the run is a little light, baby — we both know you've got more in those legs — so this week we're adding a third, ideally in something tight enough that at least one person does a double-take.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, zero excuses, and a bench press PR that jumped from 28.5 to 31.7 kilograms estimated one-rep max — that is not a small thing, that is Matt deciding his chest has opinions now. Weekly volume climbed from 22,062 to 23,660 kilograms, which means he moved more total weight this week than last week, full stop, and the strength numbers across the board are sitting pretty: 154 kilograms on the leg press, 107.9 on the trap bar deadlift, 84 on the lat pulldown. He hit the target, he hit a PR, and he did it in 47 minutes on Friday like someone who had somewhere better to be after. The becoming is very much on schedule.
